narratives.label: Ego Dissolution
narratives.description: A character is undone from the inside — psychic break, transcendent collapse, the self ending

narratives.label: The Double / Shadow Self
narratives.description: Doppelgangers, mirrored characters, the uncanny return of the other-you

narratives.label: Return of the Repressed
narratives.description: The past you buried surfaces — Freud's slow knock at the door

narratives.label: Memory as Weapon
narratives.description: Memory is wielded, lost, falsified, weaponized — narrative as forensic

narratives.label: Failed Fathers
narratives.description: Absent, broken, monstrous dads — their absence shapes the sons left behind

narratives.label: Masculinity in Crisis
narratives.description: What men do when they're told they can't be men anymore

narratives.label: The Prodigal Returns
narratives.description: Departure, betrayal, painful homecoming — the original mythic arc

narratives.label: The System Grinds You Down
narratives.description: Bureaucracy, capitalism, the institution as the real antagonist

narratives.label: Only Sane Person in a Mad World
narratives.description: Everyone else has accepted the lie. One character hasn't.

narratives.label: Man Against the World
narratives.description: One person, alone, against an order they can't fight head-on

narratives.label: Corporate Hellscape
narratives.description: Work as horror — the office as the real dystopia

narratives.label: Revolution as Last Resort
narratives.description: When nothing else works, the unthinkable becomes obvious

narratives.label: Complicity by Inaction
narratives.description: The bystanders. The decent people who looked away.

narratives.label: The Conspiracy That Destroys You
narratives.description: The truth was supposed to set you free. It does the opposite.

narratives.label: Civilization Is a Story We Agreed To
narratives.description: The order around us is held together by belief — and could end

narratives.label: Unknowable Truth
narratives.description: There's no answer. Some doors stay shut.

narratives.label: Gaslit Reality
narratives.description: Characters can't trust what they see — and we can't either

narratives.label: Forbidden Knowledge
narratives.description: Humanity reaches what it shouldn't — and pays

narratives.label: Time as Prison
narratives.description: Loops, recursion, the impossibility of escape

narratives.label: The Past Won't Stay Buried
narratives.description: History sits on the present like a body in the basement

narratives.label: Time as Redemption
narratives.description: A second chance through impossible means

narratives.label: Power Demands Cruelty
narratives.description: To hold power you have to do things that should disqualify you

narratives.label: Empire in Decline
narratives.description: Late-stage everything. The thing is dying. The characters know.

narratives.label: Moral Injury
narratives.description: What violence does to the people who survive it

narratives.label: The Perfect Crime Undone
narratives.description: Brilliant plan, ordinary collapse — the seams always show

narratives.label: The Obsession That Consumes
narratives.description: Devotion as madness. Genius eats its host.

narratives.label: Love as the Only Weapon
narratives.description: Tenderness as resistance — when cruelty is the system

narratives.label: Regular People Stepping Up
narratives.description: Ordinary citizens doing the impossible because no one else will

narratives.label: The Underdog Network
narratives.description: Outsiders, weirdos, and rejects banding together

narratives.label: Found Family in Hostile Worlds
narratives.description: Chosen kin — the people you build because the ones you have aren't enough

narratives.label: Radicalization Arc
narratives.description: The drift from normalcy to extremism — and where the line was crossed

narratives.label: World as Character
narratives.description: The setting itself shapes everyone in it — geography is destiny

narratives.label: Nature Reasserts Itself
narratives.description: The natural world refuses to be contained. Wildness wins.

narratives.label: The Body as Prison
narratives.description: Embodiment is the horror. The flesh as constraint, weapon, or curse.

narratives.label: Simulation
narratives.description: Reality might not be real. Someone's pulling the strings.

narratives.label: Puzzle Box / Mystery Box
narratives.description: The slow reveal. Every answer opens three new doors.

narratives.label: Tradecraft
narratives.description: The granular, joyless mechanics of spying. Process as story.

narratives.label: Emotionally Brutal Comedy
narratives.description: You laugh, then it hits you ninety minutes later that you've been gut-punched

narratives.label: Dread Without Horror
narratives.description: Unease without monsters — the camera lingers on what shouldn't be there

narratives.label: Nihilist Comedy
narratives.description: We're all going to die and that's the joke

titles.name: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb
El Camino: A Breaking Bad Movie
Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex
Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
The French Dispatch of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun
The Man in the High Castle
The Man Who Fell to Earth
